content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

September 25, 2007

Lori Macagni
2543 South Muirfield Court
Santa Maria, CA 93455

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-07-156

Dear Ms. Macagni:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ This letter is based on the facts presented; the Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act as the finder of fact when it renders advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.) Also, please note that our advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. We therefore offer no opinion on the applicability, if any, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflict of interest or Government Code Section 1090.

QUESTION

Does your involvement with the Angel Group of Hope present any potential conflicts of interest with your decision-making for the Santa Maria Cemetery District?

CONCLUSION

No. Under the Act, you have no conflict of interest that arises from these positions.

FACTS

In 2006, you began an effort to bring an "Angel of Hope" statue to the Santa Maria Cemetery. You proposed to the Santa Maria Cemetery District Board ("the Board") that the Board approve the Angel of Hope Project, and allow the Angel of Hope Group to direct the statue, which it did.¹ The purpose of the project is to allow parents who have lost a child to contribute memorial bricks in their child's name, to create a place for mourning and reflection for the parents, and to create community and support among these families. The funds derived from selling memorial bricks support the project. You

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-07-156
Page No. 2

are a Board Member and former President of the non-profit Angel of Hope group,
currently on a leave of absence. Your time and efforts to create a local Angel of Hope
organization are on a volunteer-basis.

In January of 2007, you were appointed to fill a vacancy on the Board. The Board
meets twice a month, and you receive a $100 stipend per meeting. You receive no other
economic benefit from either position.

There is currently a dispute between the Board and the Angel of Hope Group
regarding maintenance and preservation of the Angel of Hope Project and the on-going
sale of memorial bricks. The parties have begun to negotiate a memorandum of
understanding ("MOU") to clarify the rights and obligations of each party. You have
stated that you do not participate when the Board votes on items affecting the Angel of
Hope project. You have now taken a leave of absence from your position with the Angel
of Hope Group, and the Board has tabled the discussion regarding the MOU pending our
advice.

ANALYSIS

The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials "perform
their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests
or the financial interests of persons who have supported them." (Section 81001(b).)
Specifically, Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in
making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental
decision in which the official has a financial interest.

A public official has a "financial interest" in a governmental decision within the
meaning of the Act if it is reasonably foreseeable that the governmental decision will
have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on
the official, a member of his or her immediate family, or on any one of five enumerated
economic interests. (Section 87103; Regulation 18700(a).) The applicable economic
interests include:

1. An interest in a business entity in which a public official has a
direct or indirect investment of $2,000 or more. (Section 87103(a),
Regulation 18703.1(a).) An interest in any business entity in
which a public official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management. (Section
87103(d), Regulation 18703.1(b).)

2. An interest in real property in which a public official has a
direct or indirect interest of $2,000 or more. (Section 87103(b),
Regulation 18703.2.)

3. Any source of income, including promised income, to the
public official that aggregates to $500 or more within 12 months
prior to the decision. (Section 87103(c), Regulation 18703.3.)
"Income" does not include salary or per diem payments received
from a local agency. (Section 82030(b)(2).)

File No. A-07-156
Page No. 3

4. Any source of gifts to the public official if the gifts aggregate
to $390 or more within 12 months prior to the decision. (Section
87103(e), Regulation 18703.4.)

5. A public official also has an economic interest in his or her
personal expenses, income, assets, or liabilities, as well as those of
his or her immediate family. This is also known as the "personal
financial effects" rule. (Section 87103, Regulation 18703.5.)

Because the Act regulates only financial conflicts of interest, based on the facts
you provided regarding your positions with the Board and the Angel of Hope Group, you
do not currently have a conflict of interest, as you do not have an economic interest under
the Act.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Heather M. Rowan
Counsel, Legal Division

HMR:jgl

positions.
